review_findings.detail: parseContactCard branched on a lowercase 'npub1' prefix only, regressing uppercase NPUB1… (QR alphanumeric mode) that the old normaliseNpubPayload path accepted. Made npub detection case-insensitive in contactCard.ts + added an AC-PARSE-1 uppercase regression test.

The shared NpubQrScanner now accepts a card QR in the group-invite box, which inviteByNpub rejects as invalid_npub until S5 makes InviteMemberModal card-aware. Closed within this same delivery by S5; DD-8 safe (no cache write on the invite path).

processContactInput maps a signature-failure to the 'invalid_npub' copy — accurate as add-FAILURE (AC-PARSE-4) but the message conflates integrity failure with a malformed identifier. Product-copy nicety, non-blocking.

mocks.name: idb-keyval (Map-backed) in addContactCardWiring.test.ts
mocks.reason: AddContactModal.tsx imports useMarmot from MarmotContext, whose transitive dependency groupStorage.ts calls idb-keyval's createStore() at module load time. Reusing the exact no-op-store pattern already established by dmMessageEdits.test.ts / groupStorage.test.ts (not a new pattern) so importing the component module for its exported pure function (processContactInput) doesn't touch real IndexedDB.

mocks.name: @/src/lib/ndkClient (getNdk/connectNdk) in addContactCardWiring.test.ts
mocks.reason: Same transitive-import concern as above; stubs the NDK entry points (reusing the contactCardImport.test.ts pattern) so nothing in the import chain can attempt a real relay connection during the test run.
